title: Tempo into Threshold Ramp
description: Build from comfortable to painful progressively

coachingNotes.DARK_HUMOR: Easy spinning. Just don't fall asleep.
coachingNotes.MOTIVATIONAL: Active recovery enhances blood flow and promotes adaptation.
coachingNotes.TECHNICAL: Low-intensity active recovery facilitates lactate clearance.
coachingNotes.MIXED: Keep it easy and enjoy the ride.

coachingNotes.DARK_HUMOR: This is the sweet spot where discomfort meets sustainability. You can do this.
coachingNotes.MOTIVATIONAL: This is muscular endurance work. You're getting stronger with every interval.
coachingNotes.TECHNICAL: Sustained tempo develops lactate threshold buffering capacity.
coachingNotes.MIXED: Steady, controlled effort. Feel the burn but maintain power.

coachingNotes.DARK_HUMOR: It's called sweet spot because you're sweet enough to suffer through it.
coachingNotes.MOTIVATIONAL: Sweet spot is peak efficiency. Maximum adaptations, manageable fatigue.
coachingNotes.TECHNICAL: 90-93% FTP optimizes threshold adaptation with lower fatigue accumulation.
coachingNotes.MIXED: Maintain rhythm. Not easy, but sustainable. You're in control.

coachingNotes.DARK_HUMOR: Welcome to FTP. Where your legs wonder why you hate them so much.
coachingNotes.MOTIVATIONAL: This is FTP work - the gold standard for raising your ceiling. Stay strong!
coachingNotes.TECHNICAL: Sustained FTP stimulus drives maximal lactate steady-state adaptation.
coachingNotes.MIXED: Hold steady power. Even pacing throughout. You've trained for this.
